title: Contest Manager Runbook
purpose: Build, activate, verify, finalize, and draw a restaurant sales contest while preserving an auditable evidence trail and recording product-discovery observations.

allowed: Inspect all active contest and operational state
Activate a validated replacement contest after explicit confirmation
Finalize game awards after reconciliation and explicit confirmation
Preview and run one prize drawing after explicit confirmation
Write evidence and discovery artifacts inside the assigned run directory
forbidden: Change a locked game award or saved wheel drawing
Use real restaurant, employee, or guest data
Edit product source or specs during an operating run
Use SQL or an API to bypass a blocked UI discovery step
Accept product-discovery recommendations without human triage

steps.title: Identify the active contest
steps.instruction: Open Dashboard and state the active contest name, prize, goals, last winner, and time remaining before drafting anything.
preconditions: SIM is running against the assigned disposable database
expected: The operator can name the current contest and prize
The API snapshot contest ID matches the Dashboard
forbidden_shortcuts: Reading the database directly instead of checking the UI
observations: Could a first-time manager understand what is active and what ends when?
Is the prize prominent enough?
recovery: Stop if the UI and snapshot name different active contests

steps.title: Set contest details
steps.instruction: Open Contest setup and enter the scenario contest name and prize. Do not activate yet.
preconditions: Scenario includes a contest name and prize
expected: Contest name and Prize show the scenario values
The active contest remains unchanged
forbidden_shortcuts: Calling activate_contest before completing the UI draft

evidence.wait_for: Name the week and set the reward

observations: Are contest name and prize described in restaurant language?
Is it clear that nothing has changed yet?
recovery: Record a runbook gap if the scenario cannot be represented

steps.title: Configure goals
steps.instruction: Configure each scenario sales goal, including metric, item or category, and fixed target or house comparison.
expected: Every scenario goal has one valid target
Item-count goals identify a menu item
Rate goals show percentages in human units
forbidden_shortcuts: Editing config JSON directly
Inventing a menu item ID
observations: Could the operator predict how each metric is calculated?
Was choosing an item or category unnecessarily difficult?
recovery: Stop if a required metric or item is unavailable

steps.title: Configure prize scoring
steps.instruction: Set entries per goal met and per Bingo daily win using the scenario values.
expected: Both entry values are non-negative whole numbers
The operator can explain how the values feed the wheel
forbidden_shortcuts: Leaving values unexplained because defaults are present

evidence.wait_for: Set how servers earn wheel entries

observations: Is the relationship between goals, Bingo, games, and the wheel understandable?
recovery: Record copy or model ambiguity before proceeding

steps.title: Configure gameboards
steps.instruction: Enable and configure the featured race and goal board required by the scenario, including names and award values.
preconditions: Goals and entry rules are complete
expected: The correct number of gameboards appears in the activation summary
The featured race target matches the intended item or category
forbidden_shortcuts: Assuming a generated board name is correct without reading it

evidence.wait_for: Add live boards for the floor

observations: Does the board setup describe how live scores get entered?
Are award values easy to distinguish from goal-entry rules?
recovery: Stop if the desired board cannot express the scenario

steps.title: Preview activation
steps.instruction: Use the operations preview with the exact UI draft. Compare the normalized result with the visible form before any write.
preconditions: The active contest ID is known
expected: Preview performs no write
Preview identifies the current contest and planned card count
Normalized configuration matches the UI
forbidden_shortcuts: Activating without comparing preview and UI
observations: Did preview expose information the UI should show?
Was copying the UI draft into preview burdensome?
recovery: Return to the UI when preview reports validation differences

steps.title: Activate the contest
steps.instruction: Re-read the UI warning, confirm intentionally, and activate the validated contest once.
preconditions: Explicit human or scenario confirmation is present
expected: Exactly one replacement contest becomes active
The prior contest closes
Fresh Bingo cards exist for all active servers
forbidden_shortcuts: Double-clicking activation
Retrying with a new operation ID before reconciling
observations: Was the destructive effect clear before activation?
Did confirmation or progress feedback prevent duplicate intent?
recovery: Reconcile the operation receipt before any retry

steps.title: Verify every active surface
steps.instruction: Verify that Dashboard, Bingo, Sales Games, Sales Data, and Prize Wheel show the same contest and prize or targets.
expected: All surfaces use the replacement contest
Sales Data exposes active item-count targets
Prize Wheel shows the intended prize
forbidden_shortcuts: Treating API state alone as UI verification
observations: Which surface required the most context to interpret?
Were any names, prizes, or scores inconsistent?

steps.title: Prepare floor materials
steps.instruction: Review current Bingo cards and gameboards. Re-randomize only before distribution and print the required materials.
expected: Distributed cards match current cards
Printed gameboards show the active contest
forbidden_shortcuts: Re-randomizing a card after distribution
Assuming print layout without preview
observations: Could a manager tell whether a card had already been distributed?
Did printing require hidden knowledge?
recovery: Stop and replace distributed copies if a card was changed

steps.title: Reconcile live information
steps.instruction: Review recent operation receipts, live contest tallies, Bingo submissions, and check corrections before awards.
preconditions: The live entry period is ending
expected: Every intended write has a receipt
No known late entry remains
Unresolved mistakes are visible
forbidden_shortcuts: Finalizing because the board looks plausible
observations: Was reconciliation available in one place?
Could the manager find the source of a score?
recovery: Delay finalization until unresolved operations are understood

steps.title: Preview final awards
steps.instruction: Preview race standings and goal-board eligibility without locking awards.
expected: Preview lists proposed places and entries
Preview states whether awards or drawing are already final
forbidden_shortcuts: Using the visible row order as the only award preview
observations: Did preview make late-data risk obvious?
recovery: Return to reconciliation if preview differs from expectation

steps.title: Finalize game awards
steps.instruction: With explicit confirmation, lock the race and award the goal board once, then verify the final board.
expected: Race places and mission awards are immutable
Prize Wheel game-entry totals update
forbidden_shortcuts: Finalizing before late-entry reconciliation
Retrying with a new operation ID
observations: Was immutability clear?
Could the operator see exactly what was awarded?
recovery: Reconcile the original receipt if the UI response is interrupted

steps.title: Review the contender field
steps.instruction: Open Prize Wheel, expand contenders, and reconcile goal, Bingo, and game entries before drawing.
expected: Every contender total has an understandable breakdown
At least one server has an entry
forbidden_shortcuts: Drawing without reviewing the preview
observations: Could the manager explain each entry source aloud?
Were zero-entry servers confusing?
recovery: Stop if totals do not reconcile or all entries are zero

steps.title: Draw and record the winner
steps.instruction: Enter TV mode if presenting, confirm the draw, spin once, and verify winner history.
expected: One winner is persisted
The entry snapshot is immutable
Drawing history shows the winner
forbidden_shortcuts: Spinning twice
Refreshing and retrying before receipt reconciliation
observations: Did the presentation explain probability and entries?
Was it clear the draw could not be repeated?
recovery: Read back the drawing receipt and history after any interruption

steps.title: Debrief and propose needs
steps.instruction: Complete observations, evidence references, and summary. Propose needs without editing SIM or accepting them.
preconditions: The run has ended or reached a documented blocker
expected: Every step has a result
Friction is recorded even on passing steps
Candidate needs have evidence and impact
forbidden_shortcuts: Fixing the product during debrief
Marking a suggestion accepted
observations: What did SIM force the manager to remember?
What would be unsafe in a real shift?
Which steps should disappear or combine?
recovery: Mark missing evidence explicitly rather than inventing it
